target.name: Dyna Robotics
target.founder: Lindon Gao, York Yang, Jason Ma

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $120M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Dyna Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $120M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable in the robotics sector, spanning stages from Series A through later rounds.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $120M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $240M. Peer-median analysis identifies 7 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B; the median across this set is $1.6B. The identified peers include Foundation Robotics ($3.0B, Series A), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Collaborative Robotics ($500M, Series B).

The triangulated valuation range spans $204M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $945M. The wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity within the peer set—particularly the $10.0B outlier of 1X Technologies and the $300M floor—indicating stage and business model variation. Stage-progression suggests a floor closer to $240M, while peer comparables anchor toward the higher range. Key uncertainties include product maturity relative to Series B peers and whether Dyna's capital efficiency and burn trajectory align more closely with earlier or later stage cohorts in the robotics space.
